title: U.S.-Iran Deal’s Vague Language Comes Back to Haunt Peace Efforts
summary: The deal called for Iran to “make arrangements” for the passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has interpreted that to mean it can designate which routes ships take.

title: Deal With Israel Divides Lebanese, Fueling Protests in Beirut
summary: Supporters welcomed the agreement as a step toward curtailing Iran’s influence within Lebanon, but others took to the streets, calling it a capitulation.

title: Trump Picks Lance Schroyer as New ICE Director
summary: The president said he was nominating Lance Schroyer, an adviser to Markwayne Mullin, the D.H.S. secretary, to lead the high-profile agency.

title: Now It’s Fireworks and a Fence Keeping Tourists From the Reflecting Pool
summary: Chain-link fencing now surrounds the pool in preparation for a Fourth of July celebration. It has not stopped people from gawking.

title: Julia Letlow, Lifted by Trump, Wins Republican Senate Runoff in Louisiana
summary: If she reaches the Senate, Ms. Letlow would become the first G.O.P. woman to represent Louisiana in the chamber.

title: Pro-Transgender Candidate Is Chased From a Trans Rights Event Over Gaza
summary: Scott Wiener, who is running to replace Nancy Pelosi, was surrounded by a small group of protesters shouting invective before a transgender march in San Francisco.

title: Robby Hoffman Will Always Feel Poor, No Matter How Rich She Gets
summary: The comedian and actor says class and the way she grew up inform everything about the way she lives now.

title: We Crunched the Data: There’s a Grocery Price Emergency in America
summary: We dug into the data, toured the country and ran surveys to find the biggest financial pressures people feel.

title: In Cuba, Where So Many Have Left, These Artists Have Stayed
summary: Four young Cubans grapple with what it means to stay on an island facing unprecedented migration.

title: At Least 4 Dead in Kentucky Flooding, Governor Says
summary: Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky said emergency crews were rescuing people from flooded homes and vehicles on Saturday. Rain was expected to continue into the night.
